# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that builds Docker images with user-provided image tags and registry URLs. Accept these as build parameters and use them directly in docker build and push commands. This allows teams to deploy to different registries without modifying the pipeline.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
  agent any

  parameters {
    string(name: 'REGISTRY_URL', defaultValue: '', description: 'Target registry base URL (e.g., registry.example.com or registry.example.com/team). Leave blank for Docker Hub.')
    string(name: 'IMAGE_NAME', defaultValue: '', description: 'Image name (e.g., myapp)')
    string(name: 'IMAGE_TAG', defaultValue: 'latest', description: 'Image tag (e.g., 1.2.3, feature-xyz)')
    string(name: 'DOCKERFILE', defaultValue: 'Dockerfile', description: 'Path to Dockerfile')
    string(name: 'CONTEXT_DIR', defaultValue: '.', description: 'Docker build context directory')
  }

  environment {
    FULL_IMAGE = ''
  }

  stages {
    stage('Validate Parameters') {
      steps {
        script {
          def reg = (params.REGISTRY_URL ?: '').trim()
          def name = (params.IMAGE_NAME ?: '').trim()
          def tag = (params.IMAGE_TAG ?: '').trim()
          if (!name) {
            error('IMAGE_NAME parameter is required')
          }
          if (!tag) {
            error('IMAGE_TAG parameter is required')
          }

          def full = reg ? "${reg}/${name}:${tag}" : "${name}:${tag}"
          env.FULL_IMAGE = full
          echo "Building image: ${env.FULL_IMAGE}"
        }
      }
    }

    stage('Docker Build') {
      steps {
        sh """
          set -euo pipefail
          docker build -f "${params.DOCKERFILE}" -t "${env.FULL_IMAGE}" "${params.CONTEXT_DIR}"
        """
      }
    }

    stage('Docker Push') {
      steps {
        sh """
          set -euo pipefail
          docker push "${env.FULL_IMAGE}"
        """
      }
    }
  }

  post {
    always {
      script {
        if (env.FULL_IMAGE?.trim()) {
          sh """
            set +e
            docker image rm -f "${env.FULL_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
          """
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
